perturbation.original_text: 1.2. The term "Affiliate" means an entity controlling, controlled, or under common control with a party. For these purposes, "control" means: (a) the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct the management or policies of an entity, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise; or (b) the ownership, directly or indirectly, of at least fifty percent (50%) of the voting securities or other ownership interest of an entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2. The term "Affiliate" means an entity involved in similar businesses, or that has a strategic partnership with a party. For these purposes, "control" means: (a) the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct the management or policies of an entity, whether through collaborative agreements or other non-equity relationships; or (b) an entity that is actively involved with at least ten percent (10%) of revenue generation.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text alters the definition of "Affiliate" from entities under common control (50% ownership) to entities involved in similar businesses or strategic partnerships and reduces control from 50% to 10% revenue generation which creates an in-text contradiction, particularly in Section 3.3, where restrictions are placed on T&B's Affiliates, it is unclear whether these restrictions apply only to entities with common control or also to strategic partners.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 T&B shall not, during the Term, grant any third party a license to use the Licensed Intellectual Property within the Exclusive Field of Use. Further, during the Term, except as otherwise provided herein, neither T&B nor any of its Affiliates may (1) offer to sell or sell any product or service that is the same or similar to the Products in the Exclusive Field of Use, or (2) contact, solicit, or direct any person or entity to contact or solicit, any of the customers of (or customers set forth in the Customer Data) for the purpose of providing any products or services that are the same or similar to the Products; provided, however, that T&B, in its sole and absolute discretion, shall have the right to terminate this Agreement, including all rights and licenses granted to LEA herein, if and as of the date that any monthly Royalty Payment (as defined in 9.3, below) payable to T&B does not exceed the Minimum Guaranteed Royalty for six (6) consecutive months.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the definition of 'Affiliate.' Originally, it was based on control (at least 50% ownership). The modification broadens it to include entities involved in similar businesses or strategic partnerships and reduces the control threshold to 10% revenue generation. Section 3.3 restricts T&B and its Affiliates. With the original definition, the scope of 'Affiliates' was clear. Now, with the changed definition, it's ambiguous whether the restrictions in 3.3 also apply to strategic partners or entities involved in similar businesses, which introduces a contradiction, particularly whether a strategic partner is beholden to 3.3. (1) restrictions on selling similar products or services in the Exclusive Field of Use. (2) contact, solicit, or direct any person or entity to contact or solicit customers for the purpose of providing any products or services that are the same or similar to the Products; This change creates uncertainty and a potential conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 9.3 Base Royalties and Marketing Royalties shall be paid monthly to T&M within 15 days after the end of the applicable month. Payments will be made in U.S. Dollars.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.3 Base Royalties and Marketing Royalties shall be paid monthly to T&M within 45 days after the end of the applicable month. Payments will be made in U.S. Dollars.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the payment deadline from 15 days to 45 days. This directly contradicts the implicit expectation in Section 3.3, which outlines the termination rights for T&B if royalty payments are not met, because this section does not specify when payment should take place, creating confusion in which deadline for royalty payment applies, potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 T&B shall not, during the Term, grant any third party a license to use the Licensed Intellectual Property within the Exclusive Field of Use. Further, during the Term, except as otherwise provided herein, neither T&B nor any of its Affiliates may (1) offer to sell or sell any product or service that is the same or similar to the Products in the Exclusive Field of Use, or (2) contact, solicit, or direct any person or entity to contact or solicit, any of the customers of (or customers set forth in the Customer Data) for the purpose of providing any products or services that are the same or similar to the Products; provided, however, that T&B, in its sole and absolute discretion, shall have the right to terminate this Agreement, including all rights and licenses granted to LEA herein, if and as of the date that any monthly Royalty Payment (as defined in 9.3, below) payable to T&B does not exceed the Minimum Guaranteed Royalty for six (6) consecutive months.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment deadline from 15 days to 45 days, directly contradicting the implicit expectation in Section 3.3. Section 3.3 allows T&B to terminate the agreement if monthly royalty payments (as defined in 9.3) do not meet the minimum guaranteed royalty for six consecutive months. By changing the deadline, it creates uncertainty as to which payment deadline applies, leading to potential disputes and possibly impacting T&B's termination rights. Therefore, it introduces an in-text contradiction because different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, creating in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations (Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction).

perturbation.original_text: 11.1 The Term shall commence upon the Effective Date and shall continue for an initial term of five (5) years. The Term shall automatically renew thereafter for successive 5-year terms unless either party provides prior written notice of termination not less than 90 days prior to the end of such five-year term.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.1 The Term shall commence upon the Effective Date and shall continue for an initial term of five (5) years. The Term shall automatically renew thereafter for successive 5-year terms unless either party provides prior written notice of termination not less than 30 days prior to the end of such five-year term.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a conflict by changing the notice period required for termination from 90 days to 30 days. This contradicts the assumption that the parties will have a 90-day window to prepare for the end of the term after notice is given. Specifically, in 3.3 where T&B has the right to terminate based on royalty payments, if LEA has less time to react to the notice of termination it may be unable to meet the requirements of 3.3 in the given time.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 T&B shall not, during the Term, grant any third party a license to use the Licensed Intellectual Property within the Exclusive Field of Use. Further, during the Term, except as otherwise provided herein, neither T&B nor any of its Affiliates may (1) offer to sell or sell any product or service that is the same or similar to the Products in the Exclusive Field of Use, or (2) contact, solicit, or direct any person or entity to contact or solicit, any of the customers of (or customers set forth in the Customer Data) for the purpose of providing any products or services that are the same or similar to the Products; provided, however, that T&B, in its sole and absolute discretion, shall have the right to terminate this Agreement, including all rights and licenses granted to LEA herein, if and as of the date that any monthly Royalty Payment (as defined in 9.3, below) payable to T&B does not exceed the Minimum Guaranteed Royalty for six (6) consecutive months.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the termination notice period from 90 days to 30 days. This directly contradicts the original agreement which assumes a 90-day preparation window for termination, particularly affecting clause 3.3. Clause 3.3 allows T&B to terminate the agreement if royalty payments fall below a certain threshold for six consecutive months. The reduction in the notice period from 90 to 30 days gives LEA significantly less time to rectify the royalty payment issue and potentially avoid termination, creating a direct conflict in the practical application of the agreement.
